title: The person I call when I'm tired
summary: Describe one person you talk to when you feel tired, and what you talk about.
topic: The person I call when I'm tired
prompt: Write 5–8 short sentences about one person you call, message, or visit when you feel tired. Use present simple. Describe what you talk about and how you feel after.

hintVocab.meaningVi: nhấc máy / trả lời điện thoại

sampleText: When I am tired, I call my aunt. She lives in another city, but she always picks up. We do not talk about big things. She asks about my breakfast. She tells me about her cat. Sometimes she sings a small piece of a song from her childhood. I do not understand all the words, but I like the sound. After ten minutes, I feel okay again. My aunt is not a doctor or a teacher, but she knows how to make a hard day softer.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Present simple for habits
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng thì hiện tại đơn (call, picks up, asks, tells) cho thói quen lặp lại.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Use the present simple (call, picks up, asks, tells) for repeated habits.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Negative present simple with 'do not'
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'I do not understand' và 'We do not talk' là dạng phủ định của hiện tại đơn.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'I do not understand' and 'We do not talk' are the negative form of the present simple.

sampleTranslationVi: Khi tôi mệt, tôi gọi cho cô của mình. Cô sống ở một thành phố khác, nhưng cô luôn nhấc máy. Chúng tôi không nói những chuyện to tát. Cô hỏi tôi đã ăn sáng chưa. Cô kể cho tôi nghe về con mèo của cô. Thỉnh thoảng cô hát một đoạn nhỏ của một bài hát từ thời thơ ấu của cô. Tôi không hiểu hết các từ, nhưng tôi thích âm thanh đó. Sau mười phút, tôi cảm thấy ổn trở lại. Cô tôi không phải bác sĩ hay giáo viên, nhưng cô biết cách làm cho một ngày khó khăn trở nên dịu nhẹ hơn.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence is correct for a regular habit?
options: When I tired, I am call my aunt.
When I am tired, I call my aunt.
When tired am I, my aunt I call.
When I will be tired, I called my aunt.
mcQuestions.explanation: Both clauses use the present simple to describe a habit; 'am' before 'tired' is correct after 'I'.
mcQuestions.hint: Both verbs should be in the present simple.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the most natural negative sentence.
options: We do not talk about big things.
We no talk about big things.
We are not talk about big things.
We don't talking about big things.
mcQuestions.explanation: Negative present simple = subject + do not + base verb.
mcQuestions.hint: How do we make 'we talk' negative in the present simple?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence sounds most natural for describing how you feel after talking to someone kind?
options: After ten minutes, I am okay feel again.
After ten minutes, I feel okay again.
I feel after ten minutes okay again.
After ten minutes, I have okay feeling.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'I feel okay' is the natural verb + adjective pattern; 'again' goes at the end.
mcQuestions.hint: Which option uses 'feel' + an adjective in the simplest way?

criticalThinkingQuestion: Who in your life listens to small things without trying to fix them? Why does that matter?
